mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to predict direction in backtests with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annualized during periods of stable inflation confirms robust manufacturing output that supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 2% coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during low-inflation periods, or the predictive IC turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction below 0.5% annualized triggers immediate market repricing of corporate revenue expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5%, or the signal shows no directional predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real interest rates, defined by federal funds rate increases outpacing CPI inflation growth, compress equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The spread between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL changes fails to predict 60-day returns, with the interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 during periods of sub-5% unemployment signals a tight labor market that boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth > 200k during UNRATE < 5% coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side weakness that reduces near-term corporate cash flow expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, with a win rate below 45% over a 3-year rolling evaluation period.
